perturbation.original_text: You shall pay to AIRSOPURE an initial franchise fee of $25,000.00 upon execution of this Agreement. You agree that the initial franchise fee represents payment for the initial grant of the Franchise rights and license granted herein, shall be fully earned upon execution of this Agreement, and the said fee will not be refunded under any circumstances unless otherwise specifically set forth herein. You must find a suitable Center site within 90 days of signing the Franchise Agreement, unless we mutually agree otherwise. If we cannot agree on a Center location, within 4 months, we may a) extend your search time, b) exchange your territory, or c) terminate your Franchise and refund up to 70% of your Franchise Fee, at our sole discretion. There are no refunds under any other circumstances.
perturbation.changed_text: You shall pay to AIRSOPURE an initial franchise fee of $25,000.00 upon execution of this Agreement. You agree that the initial franchise fee represents payment for the initial grant of the Franchise rights and license granted herein, shall be fully earned upon execution of this Agreement, and the said fee will not be refunded under any circumstances unless otherwise specifically set forth herein. You must find a suitable Center site within 90 days of signing the Franchise Agreement, unless we mutually agree otherwise. If we cannot agree on a Center location, within 4 months, we may a) extend your search time, b) exchange your territory, or c) terminate your Franchise and refund up to 70% of your **application fee**, at our sole discretion. There are no refunds under any other circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: The original text refers to the '$25,000.00' fee as a 'franchise fee', which 'will not be refunded under any circumstances'. Changing 'Franchise Fee' to 'application fee' in the conditional refund clause creates a contradiction as it implies that a portion of what was earlier explicitly stated as a non-refundable 'franchise fee' may be refundable as an 'application fee' under specific circumstances.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You shall pay to AIRSOPURE an initial franchise fee of $25,000.00 upon execution of this Agreement. You agree that the initial franchise fee represents payment for the initial grant of the Franchise rights and license granted herein, shall be fully earned upon execution of this Agreement, and the said fee will not be refunded under any circumstances unless otherwise specifically set forth herein. You must find a suitable Center site within 90 days of signing the Franchise Agreement, unless we mutually agree otherwise. If we cannot agree on a Center location, within 4 months, we may a) extend your search time, b) exchange your territory, or c) terminate your Franchise and refund up to 70% of your application fee, at our sole discretion. There are no refunds under any other circumstances.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the description of the $25,000 fee from a 'franchise fee' to an 'application fee' within the conditional refund clause. The original text explicitly states that the 'franchise fee' is non-refundable. By changing 'franchise fee' to 'application fee' in the context of a potential refund, the changed text creates a direct contradiction with the initial statement that the franchise fee is non-refundable, causing uncertainty and a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Actively promote AIRSOPURE's Products and services and exert Your best efforts to fully develop and maximize the market for AIRSOPURE's Products and services in Your Exclusive Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: Actively promote AIRSOPURE's Products and services and exert Your best efforts to fully develop and maximize the market for AIRSOPURE's Products and **goods** in Your Exclusive Territory.
perturbation.explanation: The original text refers to what the franchise sells as 'Products and services' of AIRSOPURE, but changing it to 'Products and goods' creates an uncertainty about whether the 'services' that AIRSOPURE offers are included. The definition of products does not extend to services, so, therefore, this creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Actively promote AIRSOPURE's Products and services and exert Your best efforts to fully develop and maximize the market for AIRSOPURE's Products and goods in Your Exclusive Territory.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Products and services,' while the changed text refers to 'Products and goods.' This creates a contradiction because 'services' are not 'goods,' and the contradicted text uses both terms, implying that AIRSOPURE provides both products and services. Replacing 'services' with 'goods' introduces ambiguity and potential conflict by omitting the mention of services that may be part of the contractual agreement, which is a strong contradiction. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology definition, as the interchangeable use of 'goods' and 'services' without clear definition leads to conflicting obligations regarding what AIRSOPURE provides.

perturbation.original_text: Operate the Franchise in strict conformance with AIRSOPURE's policies, procedures, standards and specifications as may be prescribed by AIRSOPURE from time to time in the Operations Manual or otherwise in writing, including without limitation all changes specified by AIRSOPURE to its system or Products.
perturbation.changed_text: Operate the Franchise in strict conformance with AIRSOPURE's policies, procedures, standards and specifications as may be prescribed by AIRSOPURE from time to time in the **Franchise Handbook** or otherwise in writing, including without limitation all changes specified by AIRSOPURE to its system or Products.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the franchisee must adhere to guidelines prescribed in the 'Operations Manual'. Changing 'Operations Manual' to 'Franchise Handbook', when only the 'Operations Manual' is defined in section 8.01, creates ambiguity about whether the franchisee is bound by another document, potentially leading to uncertainty about which rules are enforceable, which introduces a contradiction, and uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: AIRSOPURE shall provide You with one copy of AIRSOPURE's Operations Manual covering the proper operating and marketing techniques and the standards and specifications for operation of the Franchise. You agree to fully comply with the Operations Manual in its entirety as an essential aspect of Your obligations under this Agreement. Failure to so comply shall be treated as a breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies adherence to the 'Operations Manual', while the changed text replaces it with 'Franchise Handbook'. The contradicted text explicitly states compliance with the 'Operations Manual' as an essential obligation. Since the 'Franchise Handbook' is not defined, and the agreement emphasizes compliance with the 'Operations Manual', replacing it creates a contradiction because the franchisee's obligations become unclear. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, specifically when the contract 'uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.'
